title: DeepSeek, Qwen, Z.AI, OpenAI adapters are 85 percent duplicate code (Stovepipe)
description:
Current state: 

Desired state: 

Reason: 

Dimension: Architectural Alignment / DRY / SRP

Location: src/providers/openai.rs, deepseek.rs, qwen.rs, zai.rs — all implement ProviderAdapter::transform_request with the identical body `serde_json::to_value(request).map_err(|e| ProviderError::RequestFailed(e.to_string()))`.

Specific duplicates:
  - openai.rs:34-37  transform_request
  - deepseek.rs:34-36
  - qwen.rs:34-36
  - zai.rs:34-37
  Also every single adapter implements get_headers with the identical two-tuple `Authorization: Bearer {api_key}` + content-type pair (openai.rs:78-83, deepseek.rs:65-70, qwen.rs:67-72, zai.rs:83-88). The chat_endpoint method also duplicates `/v1/chat/completions` verbatim across three providers.

Violation: The four OpenAI-compatible providers (OpenAI, DeepSeek, Qwen, Z.AI — Z.AI uses /chat/completions without v1 prefix) are cosmetic variants of the same adapter. The trait abstraction breaks down: we have 4x copies of a body that differs only by which thinking parameters are sprinkled into the JSON. Adding a new OpenAI-compat provider requires copying ~80 lines of boilerplate. This is the Stovepipe anti-pattern from the QA heuristic: duplicated logic across isolated modules.

Mandated Refactor: Introduce a shared base struct `OpenAiCompatAdapter` in a new file src/providers/openai_compat.rs that holds:
  struct OpenAiCompatAdapter {
      name: &'static str,
      chat_path: &'static str,
      thinking_injector: fn(&mut Value, &ThinkingConfig, &str),  // closure for provider-specific thinking
  }
Then DeepSeek/Qwen/Z.AI/OpenAI become one-line constructors returning a configured OpenAiCompatAdapter with a dedicated thinking_injector fn. Delete openai.rs, deepseek.rs, qwen.rs, zai.rs as separate types — keep them only as module-level constructor functions. Expected reduction: ~320 lines down to ~150.

Acceptance criteria: get_adapter() in mod.rs returns OpenAiCompatAdapter variants; the four thinking_injector closures encode the only real difference. All existing tests (test_openai_passthrough, test_zai_adapter, etc.) must still pass against the unified adapter.
